name: Is my article worth suing Perplexity over? How much could I get?
description: Potentially $150K+ per willful infringement if registered; assess harm with lawyer.[2]

mainEntity.name: Is my article worth suing Perplexity over? How much could I get?

acceptedAnswer.text: Potentially $150K+ per willful infringement if registered; assess harm with lawyer.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Value depends on circulation, provable harm, and statutory damages up to $150K per willful infringement, as sought by publishers.[2] NYT alleges millions in copies; smaller creators may seek actual damages plus profits.[1][3] Key factors: Registration timely boosts awards; fair use fight. Chicago Tribune claims revenue loss from traffic diversion.[6] Calculate lost licensing/subscriptions. Nationwide applicability. IP lawyer can estimate via case comps. Many settle pre-trial.
